summary: Matched and linked melee/ft/ftdata by implementing ftData routines, moving the large common fighter motion-state tables into a new ftmotionstates.c split for data alignment, and tightening related fighter/player/library prototypes. The decomp.dev report credited 13 new matches, including 12 ftdata functions and ftKb_Init_UnkMotionStates5, improving GALE01 matched code by +2616 bytes and linked code by +2916 bytes.
what_changed: Changed configure.py so melee/ft/ftdata.c and the new melee/ft/ftmotionstates.c are built as Matching objects instead of ftdata remaining NonMatching.
Adjusted config/GALE01/splits.txt: ft_0852 no longer owns the 0x803C0EC0 data range, ftdata.c now owns data 0x803C0EC0-0x803C2800, and new ftmotionstates.c owns data 0x803C2800-0x803C5460.
Moved CostumeListsForeachCharacter and its extern costume-list references out of ft_0852.c into ftdata.c so the data belongs to the matching ftdata split.
Moved the large MotionState arrays ftData_MotionStateList and ftData_803C52A0 out of ftdata.c into new src/melee/ft/ftmotionstates.c; the PR body states this was done to satisfy data alignment.

Implemented multiple ftdata functions including ftData_80085560, ftData_800857E0, ftData_80085820, ftData_800858E4, ftData_800859A8, ftData_80085A14, ftData_80085B98, ftData_80085CD8, ftData_80085E50, and ftData_80085FD4 according to the bot match report and diff excerpt.
Implemented ftKb_Init_UnkMotionStates5 as a void callback that calls Player_80031DC8(ftKb_SpecialN_800EED50).
Updated ftData_UnkMotionStates5 from HSD_GObjEvent to Event and changed its callback invocation to no-argument function calls.
Refined player mapping declarations: ftMapping_list is indexed by CHKIND_MAX and initialized with named CKIND/CHKIND comments and FTKIND_* constants instead of raw numeric literals.
Updated Player_80031CB0 and Player_80031D2C signatures from generic enum_t/id and int slot to CharacterKind kind and u8 color, forwarding mapped FTKIND values to ftData_800855C8 and ftData_8008578C.

Tightened struct and prototype types in ft/types.h and headers, including S_TEMP4.x0 as char*, S_TEMP4.x14 as u32, Fighter.x58C as u32, Fighter.x598 as FigaTree*, Fighter.x61C as s8, Fighter_x59C_t storage as u8 x0[0x8000], ftData_UnkBytePerCharacter as u8, ftData_800855C8 parameters as FighterKind/u8, and ftData_80086060 as Fighter* -> Fighter*.
Updated lbArq_80014BD0 from UNK_RET/UNK_PARAMS to void lbArq_80014BD0(u32, void*, size_t, UNK_T, UNK_T).
smart_moves: Separated the enormous motion-state table into ftmotionstates.c rather than forcing ftdata.c to carry both executable logic and alignment-sensitive data; this let ftdata.c match while preserving the original data address boundaries.
Used splits.txt and configure.py together: the data-region split was declared explicitly, then both affected compilation units were marked Matching.
Moved data ownership from ft_0852.c to ftdata.c where the linker layout expected CostumeListsForeachCharacter, instead of leaving a matching obstacle in a neighboring source file.
Replaced raw ftMapping_list numeric IDs with FTKIND_* constants and CKIND comments, making the table more auditable while preserving the same mapping intent.

Improved callback typing by using Event for no-argument ftData_UnkMotionStates5 callbacks and changing ftKb_Init_UnkMotionStates5 to void(void), matching the actual call site that invokes table entries with no arguments.
Introduced more specific types in shared structs and headers before/while matching ftdata, reducing casts and making pointer arithmetic, archive relocation, and size fields clearer.
decomp_lessons: For large data-heavy files, a matching failure can be due to section/data alignment rather than individual initializer contents; splitting alignment-sensitive tables into their own compilation unit can be the clean solution.
When moving data between files, update both the linker split ranges and the build object matching status in the same PR so the repository layout and build graph remain consistent.
Callback arrays should be typed to the actual calling convention. Here, ftData_UnkMotionStates5 became Event because entries are invoked as no-argument callbacks.
Typed constants in tables are safer than raw numeric IDs. Rewriting ftMapping_list with CKIND comments and FTKIND_* constants preserves order while making future audit work easier.
Small header refinements can unblock matching in data/loading code: signedness of sentinel bytes, pointer-vs-integer fields, and size_t/u32 widths all affect generated code.
Evidence for author intent is limited to the PR body and diff; the only explicit rationale given was moving the motion-state table to ftmotionstates.c to satisfy data alignment.
naming_conventions: New motion-state split file is named src/melee/ft/ftmotionstates.c and keeps exported symbols ftData_MotionStateList and ftData_803C52A0.
Character mapping table comments use CKIND_* or CHKIND_* labels alongside FTKIND_* initializer constants.
Player APIs were renamed at the parameter level from generic id/slot to CharacterKind kind and u8 color.
ftdata symbols still use address-based placeholder names such as ftData_80085560 and ftData_803C52A0 where semantic names were not established.
Typed callback array uses the generic Event typedef for no-argument events, while HSD_GObjEvent remains for GObj-taking callback tables.
assembly_or_matching_tactics: Created a separate data-only compilation unit for ftData_MotionStateList and ftData_803C52A0 at data range 0x803C2800-0x803C5460 to satisfy alignment.
Reassigned CostumeListsForeachCharacter to ftdata.c at data address 0x803C0EC0, matching the split layout expected by GALE01.
Used PAD_STACK(4) in ftData_80085A14, indicating stack-shape adjustment was needed for matching.

Changed ftData_UnkBytePerCharacter from s8 to u8 even though the initializer includes -1 sentinel values, suggesting byte-level layout/codegen mattered more than signed interpretation at the declaration site.
Replaced UNK_RET/UNK_PARAMS with concrete lbArq_80014BD0 parameters so callers in ftdata could compile to the expected ABI.
Implemented assertion/reporting strings inline in matching functions, including fighter reference counter error, fighter figatree over, Demo Status error, and lbArchiveRelocate error paths.
review_feedback: No human review comments were present in the provided slice.
decomp.dev bot reported GALE01 matched code 44.89% (+0.07%, +2616 bytes) and linked code 28.06% (+0.08%, +2916 bytes).
decomp.dev bot listed 13 new matches: 12 in main/melee/ft/ftdata and ftKb_Init_UnkMotionStates5 in ftKirby/ftKb_Init.
follow_up_queries: Find later PRs that rename ftData_80085560 or other newly matched ftdata functions to semantic names.
Check whether ftData_UnkBytePerCharacter's u8 declaration with -1 sentinel initializers received later cleanup or comments.
Look for follow-up PRs that further split fighter data tables or reuse the ftmotionstates.c data-alignment pattern.
Audit later changes to ftMapping_list for the CKIND_MAX versus FTKIND_MAX indexing distinction.
Search for later prototype refinements around lbArq_80014BD0, Player_80031CB0, and Player_80031D2C.
